topic.what: Secure evaluation of general non-linear functions (multiplication, power, exp, trig, sigmoid, softmax) in constant-round MPC
topic.how: Mask-Broadcast-Unmask pattern using precomputed random tuples enabling local evaluation on reconstructed masked values
topic.why_matters: Removes final barrier for practical privacy-preserving ML; achieves exact computation without polynomial or quantization approximation errors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of synchronous communication rounds per function evaluation
characteristics.vs_baseline: log2(k) for k-party M2A; O(1)-O(log n) for polynomial approximations
characteristics.context: Algebraic functions: 1 round; transcendental: 4-6 rounds (3 for 2 parties)

characteristics.value: O(kn) for k-variable multiplication; O(n) for others
characteristics.vs_baseline: Linear scaling like other constant-round protocols
characteristics.context: Linear in number of parties n

characteristics.what_it_measures: Deviation from plaintext evaluation due to protocol
characteristics.vs_baseline: Polynomial: up to 11 ULP; FSS: quantization error
characteristics.context: No polynomial fitting or LUT quantization errors
Full scalability; no dependency on n
Information-theoretic security via additive secret sharing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of non-linear function families supported
characteristics.value: 7 (multiplication, power, exp, sin, cos, sigmoid, softmax)
characteristics.context: Most comprehensive coverage among MPC solutions
